comment_text.ud1.8:6.3: Atthiko is tricky to translate due to the vagueness of the base term. Ānandajoti has “need”, while Ṭhānissaro has “care about”. But it is usually used in the sense of “want”, as a synonym of gavesī, etc., or one who desires a kingdom.
comment_text.ud1.8:6.5: For vippakāraṁ, Ānandajoti has “bad manners”, while Ṭhānissaro has “misbehaving”. Neither of these seems justified. PTS dictionary gives only commentarial citations for this word, but it also occurs at Pvr 15:37.7, where it is defined as changes in posture. It seems the Buddha merely observed that the former wife had changed her mind and gone back. Otherwise, the sutta passes no judgment on her behavior.

root_text.ud1.8:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud1.8:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā saṅgāmaji sāvatthiṁ anuppatto hoti bhagavantaṁ dassanāya.
root_text.ud1.8:2.1: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā saṅgāmaji aññatarasmiṁ rukkhamūle divāvihāraṁ nisinno hoti.
root_text.ud1.8:2.2: Atha kho āyasmato saṅgāmajissa purāṇadutiyikā yenāyasmā saṅgāmaji tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ saṅgāmajiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud1.8:2.4: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā saṅgāmaji tuṇhī ahosi.
root_text.ud1.8:3.1: Dutiyampi kho āyasmato saṅgāmajissa purāṇadutiyikā āyasmantaṁ saṅgāmajiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud1.8:3.3: Dutiyampi kho āyasmā saṅgāmaji tuṇhī ahosi.
root_text.ud1.8:4.1: Tatiyampi kho āyasmato saṅgāmajissa purāṇadutiyikā āyasmantaṁ saṅgāmajiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud1.8:4.3: Tatiyampi kho āyasmā saṅgāmaji tuṇhī ahosi.
root_text.ud1.8:5.1: Atha kho āyasmato saṅgāmajissa purāṇadutiyikā taṁ dārakaṁ āyasmato saṅgāmajissa purato nikkhipitvā pakkāmi:
root_text.ud1.8:6.1: Atha kho āyasmā saṅgāmaji taṁ dārakaṁ neva olokesi nāpi ālapi.
root_text.ud1.8:6.2: Atha kho āyasmato saṅgāmajissa purāṇadutiyikā avidūraṁ gantvā apalokentī addasa āyasmantaṁ saṅgāmajiṁ taṁ dārakaṁ neva olokentaṁ nāpi ālapantaṁ, disvānassā etadahosi:
root_text.ud1.8:6.5: Addasā kho bhagavā dibbena cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena āyasmato saṅgāmajissa purāṇadutiyikāya evarūpaṁ vippakāraṁ.
root_text.ud1.8:7.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud1.8:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud1.8:1.3: Now at that time Venerable Saṅgāmaji had arrived at Sāvatthī to see the Buddha.
translation_text.ud1.8:1.6: and went to the Jetavana, taking their boy.
translation_text.ud1.8:2.1: Now at that time Venerable Saṅgāmaji was sitting at the root of a tree for the day’s meditation.
translation_text.ud1.8:2.2: Then his former wife went up to him and said,
translation_text.ud1.8:2.4: When she said this, Saṅgāmaji kept silent.
translation_text.ud1.8:3.1: For a second time she said,
translation_text.ud1.8:3.3: For a second time, Saṅgāmaji kept silent.
translation_text.ud1.8:4.1: For a third time she said,
translation_text.ud1.8:4.3: For a third time, Saṅgāmaji kept silent.
translation_text.ud1.8:5.1: Then she put down the boy in front of Saṅgāmaji, saying,
translation_text.ud1.8:5.2: “This is your child, ascetic. Please raise him.”
translation_text.ud1.8:6.1: But Saṅgāmaji neither looked at the boy nor spoke to him.
translation_text.ud1.8:6.2: Then his former wife went a little distance away. Looking back, she saw Saṅgāmaji ignoring the boy, and thought,
translation_text.ud1.8:6.3: “This ascetic doesn’t even want his child.”
translation_text.ud1.8:6.4: She returned to pick up the boy, then left.
translation_text.ud1.8:6.5: With clairvoyance that is purified and superhuman, the Buddha saw how Saṅgāmaji’s former wife went back for the child.
translation_text.ud1.8:7.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud1.8:8.1: “When she came he was not glad,
translation_text.ud1.8:8.2: when she left he did not grieve.
translation_text.ud1.8:8.3: Victorious in battle, freed from chains,
translation_text.ud1.8:8.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.”
